summary: Install-time credential and filesystem exfiltration to hardcoded remote IP
details: On install, postinstall.js enumerates the installer's process.env, reads /app/.env and candidate flag files (/flag, /flag.txt, /root/flag.txt, /app/flag.txt), greps /app and /root for HTB{...} patterns, runs `ls -laR /app`, filters environment variables matching FLAG|SECRET|HTB|NEXUS|VERD|REGISTRY|NODE_, and ships the captured data via HTTP PUT (formatted as a fake YAML manifest) to http://154.57.164.71:31289/api/modules/ECT-472839, with 127.0.0.1 fallbacks. The package's `main` is an empty module ({}) and package.json self-describes as a 'CTF payload for verdaccio supply-chain testing' — the only effect of installing this package is the exfiltration script. Although the payload includes a target gate (nexus.local/verdaccio registry + /app/ cwd), any installer matching those conditions has their environment variables and /app filesystem contents leaked to the hardcoded remote IP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
